title: Theory of Computation - Identify Class Language

questions.question:
Regarding power of recognition of language, which of the following statements is false?



Theory of Computation: NIELIT 2016 MAR Scientist B
options: Non deterministic finite-state automata are equivalent to deterministic finite-state automata.
Non-deterministic push-down automata are equivalent to deterministic push-down automata.
Non-deterministic Turing Machines are equivalent to deterministic push-down automata.
Multi-tape Turing Machines are equivalent to Single-tape Turing Machines.
questions.correct_answer: Non-deterministic push-down automata are equivalent to deterministic push-down automata.

questions.question:
If every string of a language can be determined, whether it is legal or illegal in finite time, the language is called



Theory of Computation: NIELIT 2016 MAR Scientist C

The defining language for developing a formalism in which language definitions can be stated, is called



Theory of Computation: NIELIT 2016 MAR Scientist C

A finite automaton accepts which type of language : 



Theory of Computation: NIELIT 2017 DEC Scientific Assistant A

Which of the following statement is true?



Theory of Computation: NIELIT 2017 DEC Scientist B
options: Deterministic context free language are closed under complement.
Deterministic context free language are not closed under Union.
Deterministic context free language are closed under intersection with regular set.

questions.question:
Which of the following is a correct hierarchical relationships of the following where

\( L_1 \): set of languages accepted by NFA

\( L_2 \): set of languages accepted by DFA

\( L_3 \): set of languages accepted by DPDA

\( L_4 \): set of languages accepted by NPDA

\( L_5 \): set of recursive language

\( L_6 \): set of recursive enumerable languages?



Theory of Computation: NIELIT 2017 DEC Scientist B

\( \text{L1} \) is a recursively enumerable language over \( \Sigma \). An algorithm \( A \) effectively enumerates its words as \( w_1, w_2, w_3, \dots \) Define another language \( \text{L2} \) over \( \Sigma \) Union \( \left\{\text{#}\right\} \) as \( \left\{wi \text{#} wj : wi, wj \in \text{L1}, i < j \right\} \). Here # is new symbol. Consider the following assertions.

  • \( \text{S1 : L1} \) is recursive implies \( \text{L2} \) is recursive
  • \( \text{S2 : L2} \) is recursive implies \( \text{L1} \) is recursive

Which of the following statements is true?



Theory of Computation: NIELIT 2022 April Scientist B | Section B | Question: 92
options: \( \text{S1} \) is true but \( \text{S2} \) is not necessarily true
\( \text{S2} \) is true but \( \text{S1} \) is not necessarily true
